Eden Framework Tutorial¶

A comprehensive, structured learning pathway from beginner to expert.

How to Use This Tutorial¶

This tutorial is organized into 8 progressive phases. Each phase builds upon the previous one, so we recommend working through them in order.

Learning Pathway¶

  • 1. Phase 1: Your First Eden Application
    • 1.1. What You’ll Learn
    • 1.2. 1.1 Installing Eden
    • 1.3. 1.2 Creating Your First App
    • 1.4. 1.3 Understanding the Code
    • 1.5. 1.4 Adding More Routes
    • 1.6. 1.5 Path Parameters
    • 1.7. 1.6 Query Parameters
    • 1.8. 1.7 Project Organization
    • 1.9. 1.8 Using the CLI
    • 1.10. 1.9 Configuration Options
    • 1.11. 1.10 Complete Example
    • 1.12. 1.11 Summary
    • 1.13. Next Steps
  • 2. Phase 2: Data Modeling
    • 2.1. What You’ll Learn
    • 2.2. 2.1 Setting Up the Database
    • 2.3. 2.2 Creating Your First Model
    • 2.4. 2.3 Field Types
    • 2.5. 2.4 Primary Keys
    • 2.6. 2.5 Creating Records
    • 2.7. 2.6 Reading Records
    • 2.8. 2.7 Updating Records
    • 2.9. 2.8 Deleting Records
    • 2.10. 2.9 Relationships
    • 2.11. 2.10 Advanced Queries
    • 2.12. 2.11 Ordering and Pagination
    • 2.13. 2.12 Aggregations
    • 2.14. 2.13 F Expressions (Atomic Updates)
    • 2.15. 2.14 Complete Example
    • 2.16. Summary
    • 2.17. Next Steps
  • 3. Phase 3: Routing & Requests
    • 3.1. What You’ll Learn
    • 3.2. 3.1 Modular Routers
    • 3.3. 3.2 Path Parameters
    • 3.4. 3.3 The Request Object
    • 3.5. 3.4 Request Body
    • 3.6. 3.5 Response Types
    • 3.7. 3.6 Status Codes
    • 3.8. 3.7 Exception Handling
    • 3.9. 3.8 Dependency Injection
    • 3.10. 3.9 WebSocket Routes
    • 3.11. 3.10 Route Groups
    • 3.12. 3.11 URL Reversing
    • 3.13. 3.12 Complete Example
    • 3.14. Summary
    • 3.15. Next Steps
  • 4. Phase 4: Forms & Validation
    • 4.1. What You’ll Learn
    • 4.2. 4.1 Pydantic Basics
    • 4.3. 4.2 Eden Form Class
    • 4.4. 4.3 Form in Routes
    • 4.5. 4.4 Validation Types
    • 4.6. 4.5 Custom Validators
    • 4.7. 4.6 File Uploads
    • 4.8. 4.7 Form Rendering
    • 4.9. 4.8 Widget Tweaks
    • 4.10. 4.9 JSON API Validation
    • 4.11. 4.10 Complete Example
    • 4.12. Summary
    • 4.13. Next Steps
  • 5. Phase 5: Authentication
    • 5.1. What You’ll Learn
    • 5.2. 5.1 User Model
    • 5.3. 5.2 Password Management
    • 5.4. 5.3 Session Authentication
    • 5.5. 5.4 Login/Logout
    • 5.6. 5.5 Protected Routes
    • 5.7. 5.6 Getting Current User
    • 5.8. 5.7 Registration
    • 5.9. 5.8 Remember Me
    • 5.10. 5.9 Complete Auth Example
    • 5.11. Summary
    • 5.12. Next Steps
  • 6. Phase 6: Permissions & Authorization
    • 6.1. What You’ll Learn
    • 6.2. 6.2 AccessControl Mixin
    • 6.3. 6.3 Permission Rules
    • 6.4. 6.4 Decorator-Based Authorization
    • 6.5. 6.5 Query-Level Filtering
    • 6.6. 6.6 Custom Permission Rules
    • 6.7. 6.7 Complete Example
    • 6.8. Summary
    • 6.9. Next Steps
  • 7. Phase 7: Templating
    • 7.1. What You’ll Learn
    • 7.2. 7.1 Template Setup
    • 7.3. 7.2 Eden Template Syntax
    • 7.4. 7.3 Conditionals
    • 7.5. 7.4 Loops
    • 7.6. 7.5 Template Context
    • 7.7. 7.6 Filters
    • 7.8. 7.7 CSRF Protection
    • 7.9. 7.8 HTMX Integration
    • 7.10. 7.9 Static Files
    • 7.11. 7.10 Complete Example
    • 7.12. Summary
    • 7.13. Next Steps
  • 8. Phase 8: Real-World Patterns
    • 8.1. What You’ll Learn
    • 8.2. 8.1 Production Configuration
    • 8.3. 8.2 Error Handling
    • 8.4. 8.3 Caching
    • 8.5. 8.4 Background Tasks
    • 8.6. 8.5 Logging
    • 8.7. 8.6 Testing
    • 8.8. 8.7 File Storage
    • 8.9. 8.8 Email
    • 8.10. 8.9 Deployment
    • 8.11. 8.10 Complete Production Example
    • 8.12. Summary
    • 8.13. Congratulations!

Phase Overview¶

Phase

Topic

Level

Description

1

First Application

Beginner

Your first Eden app, project structure, running the server

2

Data Modeling

Beginner-Intermediate

Models, fields, relationships, database operations

3

Routing & Requests

Intermediate

Routes, path parameters, request/response handling

4

Forms & Validation

Intermediate

Form handling, Pydantic validation, widget customization

5

Authentication

Advanced

User sessions, login/logout, password management

6

Permissions

Advanced

Role-based access control, authorization decorators

7

Templating

Advanced

Templates, components, HTMX integration

8

Real-World Patterns

Expert

Production patterns, optimization, deployment

What You’ll Build¶

By the end of this tutorial, you’ll have built a complete blog application with:

  • User authentication and registration

  • CRUD operations for posts

  • Role-based permissions (admin, author, reader)

  • Rich templating with components

  • Form validation and error handling

Prerequisites¶

  • Python 3.11 or higher installed

  • Basic understanding of Python

  • Familiarity with web concepts (HTTP, HTML)

Code Examples¶

All code examples in this tutorial are complete and executable. You can copy them directly into your project.

Conventions Used¶

Note

Code blocks marked with Basic show minimal implementations.

Tip

Code blocks marked with Intermediate show enhanced patterns.

Warning

Code blocks marked with Advanced show production-ready patterns.

Let’s begin with Phase 1: First Application!
